commit.message:
Remove JS text truncation in table formatters, let CSS line-clamp handle it

Removed the 60-char details truncation and 24-char secret truncation from
JS formatters — CSS 3-line clamp handles visual truncation, and the removed
JS truncation was preventing tooltip overflow detection from triggering.
Also improved tooltip handler with dual detection: JS truncation check
(tip length vs visible text) and CSS clamp check (unset clamp to measure
true scrollHeight).

commit.message:
Fix tooltip overflow detection for line-clamped cells

Previous scrollHeight check didn't work with -webkit-line-clamp.
Now temporarily unsets the clamp to measure true content height,
then compares against clamped height to decide if tooltip is needed.

commit.message:
Fix graph ordering: WEB01 after DC01, access→host edges

Pivot edges now prefer host→host (not access→host) so BFS layer
assignment places pivot destinations after the source host. Added
unconditional access→host edges so the chain flows correctly:
cred → access → host → [pivot] → next host.

commit.message:
Fix graph: WEB01 as pivot dest, table cell wrapping + tooltips

Graph fixes:
- pivotDestHosts built from actual edge rendering (replaces broken
  pivotDests built from raw destination text that never matched hosts)
- Fuzzy pivot source/destination matching: descriptive text like
  "DC01.pirate.htb (10.129.244.95) - gMSA..." now matches host IPs
  and subnet prefixes (192.168.100.0/24 matches host 192.168.100.2)
- WEB01 now correctly positioned as pivot destination from DC01 access
- Pivot edge labels truncated to first segment (≤40 chars)

commit.message:
Add via_access_id provenance tracking to kill-chain graph

Schema v3→v4 migration adds via_access_id FK on credentials and vulns
tables, linking discoveries to the access session that found them.
State-server add_credential/add_vuln accept the new parameter.
State-viewer graph uses via_access_id for explicit edge inference
(access→vuln, access→cred) with heuristic fallback for NULL values.
Access nodes are pre-created before vuln/cred processing so references
resolve correctly.

commit.message:
fix: stabilize callback refs to break pane title render loop

Inline arrow functions in WorkspaceV2TabRoute created new references
every render, causing PaneRuntimeProvider's useMemo to recompute,
which re-triggered useSetPaneTitle's useEffect, which called
setRuntimeTitleByTabId, causing an infinite re-render cycle.

- Wrap all callback props in useCallback in AuthenticatedWorkspaceHost
- Use refs for state.groups and openHintByTabId in publishTabTitle to
  keep its reference stable across workspace state changes
- Add equality bail-out in setRuntimeTitleByTabId to skip no-op updates

commit.message:
fix: reduce alert dedup window from 5min to 30s for accurate counts

Heavy barrages fire multiple ORef alerts minutes apart; the 5-minute
timestamp dedup was collapsing them into a single event. 30s is enough
to handle the historical-seed / real-time-poll overlap case.

Also normalize real-time alert IDs to match historical format
(ra_{taCityId}_{ts}) so duplicates are caught by ID before timestamp
clustering.

feat: dual-token auth, startup batch API, maintenance/update gates & deep linking

Auth:
- Migrate from single JWT to access + refresh token pair (custom tbc-ca endpoints)
- Remove stored credentials from SecureStore (refresh token handles re-auth)
- Background profile sync on init, clear user caches on logout

Performance:
- Single POST /tbc-ca/v1/batch replaces ~11 startup HTTP requests
- useCachedData gains staleTime + batch-fresh detection to skip redundant fetches
- Memoize ThemeContext and PusherContext provider values
- Tab activity wrapper using React 19 Activity component

Admin Controls:
- Force update gate blocks app below minimum version (links to app stores)
- Maintenance screen with auto-retry, admin bypass, and server-driven message
- Dynamic menu visibility via server config (hide_menu array)

Deep Linking:
- URL-to-route mapper for spaces, profiles, courses, notifications
- iOS universal links (associatedDomains) + Android app links (intentFilters)
- Queues deep link pre-auth, replays after login

Infrastructure:
- AppConfigContext for auth-aware config (maintenance, visibility, updates)
- UnreadCountsContext consolidates badge state (replaces duplicate fetches)
- Response header interceptor syncs badges + detects mid-session maintenance
- Reanimated v3 tab wobble animation replacing Animated API

Branding:
- App slug/scheme: FluentCommunityApp → TwoBirdsCommunity
- Updated EAS project ID and owner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add automatic backlink discovery — Obsidian-inspired bidirectional linking

On every /memory/learn call, discovers semantically related memories (0.4–0.84
similarity) across all domains and creates bidirectional BACKLINK edges in Neo4j.
Builds a web of connections automatically without manual linking.

- app/backlinks.py: discover_backlinks() finds related memories at ingest time
- MemoryRef nodes bridge vector IDs to graph nodes for cross-store linking
- GET /memory/{id}/backlinks endpoint to retrieve discovered connections
- LearnResponse now includes backlinks[] with discovered related memories
- 14 new tests (393 total passing)

commit.message:
feat(lsp): implement incremental text synchronization (#715)

## Summary

- Switch from full to incremental text synchronization so the client
sends only changed ranges instead of entire documents on each keystroke
- Reduces bandwidth and CPU usage, especially for large files
- Closes #275

## Changes

- Changed `TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL` to `INCREMENTAL` in server
capabilities
- Added `apply_content_change()` utility that converts LSP UTF-16
positions to byte offsets and applies range-based edits
- Added `document_contents` map to `WorkspaceManager` to maintain
in-memory document state for applying incremental edits
- Updated `did_open` to store initial content, `did_change` to apply
incremental edits, and `did_close` to clean up
- Added 8 unit tests covering full replacement, single-char insert, word
replacement, deletion, multiline, cross-line, sequential edits, and
content lifecycle

## Test Plan

1. Open a `.graphql` file in VS Code with the extension active
2. Type changes and verify real-time diagnostics still update correctly
3. Test with a large schema file to observe reduced latency on
keystrokes
4. Verify cross-file diagnostics still refresh on save (e.g., rename a
fragment and save)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement 8 gap fixes + 4 conflict resolution improvements

Gap fixes (from analysis vs Brick):
- fix(offline_first): warn when forceLocalSyncFromRemote=true without mixin
- fix(odoo): use storeRemoteResults in hydrateRemote (gap 4)
- fix(rest): use storeRemoteResults in hydrateRemote (gap 4)
- fix(supabase): use storeRemoteResults in hydrateRemote (gap 4)
- docs(odoo): clarify _transmit double-unlock semantics (gap 5)
- docs(odoo_model): document odooId mutability contract (gap 6)
- docs(sync_manager): show Option B reusing main Drift DB executor (gap 7)
- feat(ci): add REST packages to main matrix; add optional Supabase job (gap 3)
- feat(melos): add analyze-rest and analyze-supabase scripts (gap 3)
- feat(build): add build_test + generator tests scaffold (gap 1)
- chore: add pub.dev metadata to publishable packages (gap 2)

Conflict resolution improvements:
- feat(offline_first): propagate _latestGetPolicy to getAssociation
- feat(query): add applyToJoinedSelect for cross-table JOIN queries

fix: remove unsupported groups arg from deform_conv2d

Older torchvision versions do not expose the groups parameter in
deform_conv2d. Since g=1 (default) is always used in the YAML,
removing the argument has no effect on behaviour.

Anonymize case study: protect client confidentiality, keep technical depth

- Remove identifying details: company names, drug names, pharmacy chains,
  specific tools (Telegram, Odoo v19, NotebookLM, PDB)
- Generalize industry references: "farmacéutica" → "productos regulados"
- Keep all numbers, architectures, and engineering methodology intact

commit.message:
fix: use flutter build ios --no-codesign to properly embed dart defines

xcodebuildを直接呼ぶとDerivedDataキャッシュにより DART_DEFINES が
再コンパイルされずemptyになる問題を修正。
flutter build ios --no-codesign で --dart-define を直接渡す方式に変更し、
APP_PATHもFlutterビルド出力ディレクトリを優先して参照するよう更新。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set UTF-8 locale and force tmux unicode mode

Alpine container had no locale set, causing tmux client to fall back
to ASCII rendering — Unicode symbols from Claude Code's TUI showed as
underscores. Fix:
- Set LANG=C.UTF-8 in Dockerfile
- Add -u flag to all tmux commands (force UTF-8)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: cross-validated hold penalties, hysteresis, score history dashboard

Hold penalties now cross-reference L1/L2 data — if sharps also held, DK
holding is neutral; if sharps moved and DK lagged, that's a real signal.
Added decision hysteresis (exit thresholds lower than entry) to prevent
STRONG→LEAN oscillation. Score History tab tracks total_score over time
with color-coded arrows and decision movement indicators.

UI polish and new features: lead scoring, cost tracking, SMS, drip campaigns

- Unified CSS across all pages with consistent spacing, border-radius, and shadows

- Added drip campaign support with auto-retry for voicemail/no-answer
- Added call scheduling with timezone, calling hours, and day-of-week support
- Added backend auth API (JWT + bcrypt) and DNC list management
- Tightened sidebar (240px), compact cards, Inter font throughout
